anchored_threads.thread_name: The sthita-prajña / one whose insight is steady (verses 2.54-2.72) — the character of the operator who has internalized first-principles knowledge and karma-yoga discipline
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The engineering layer specifically anchors prajñā (the discriminative wisdom of the sthita-prajña block) as the cultivated faculty that is eroded by un-restrained input. The verse is positioned in the chapter's sthita-prajña block (specifically its negative case-statement) and the analog scopes prajñā downward to engineering judgment without flattening — it preserves the technical Sanskrit term verbatim ('That faculty is what the verse calls prajñā'). The state-of-the-operator focus (the senior engineer's discriminative judgment cultivated across architectural decisions and on-call rotations) is the chapter's third thread.

progression_issue.explanation: The verse sits at position 2.67 in the chapter — within the sthita-prajña block (2.54-2.72) and specifically as the negative case-statement that 2.68 will restate positively. The engineering layer's framing as 'the loss-mechanism that 2.68 will restate positively' is correct chapter-progressive — it does not preempt the chapter's terminal state (brāhmī sthiti at 2.72) and does not regress to earlier teachings. The cross-verse pattern (input-stream → eroded prajñā) builds on 2.60-2.61's sense-control teaching and is the right rung at this position.

verdict_reason: BG 2.67 anchors cleanly on the chapter's third thread (sthita-prajña / steady-mind / state of the seasoned operator). The engineering layer preserves the chapter's hard edge that 'the steady mind is a real state, not an aspiration' — the analog gives a concrete operational description of the prajñā-erosion mechanism rather than treating it as a slogan. No counter-anti-thesis drift (the verse is not teaching detachment-from-users; the falsifiability clause explicitly closes that misread). Progressive-build position is correct: 2.67 is the negative case-statement immediately preceding 2.68's positive corollary; this verse-pair structure is preserved by the analog. Pass on both 10.3 and 10.4.
